page: Amazon Web Services r5a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance provides 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, offering 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 8 MB L3 cache. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the r5a.xlarge exhibits modest compute and memory performance. In stress-ng and Geekbench tests, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 10% category. Memory bandwidth tests show weak read-only performance, with cached small blocks reaching 154.15 GB/sec and uncached large blocks at 25.43 GB/sec, while large block write bandwidth is poor at 12.49 GB/sec. However, the processor performs at an average level in cryptographic operations, as demonstrated by the PassMark CPU encryption test score of 3238.05, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instructions.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5a.xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio (8:1) on AMD EPYC hardware, which typically provides a lower-cost alternative to comparable Intel-based memory-optimized instances. This makes it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU performance, such as caching layers, medium-sized databases with low transaction rates, and general-purpose memory-intensive applications. It is less suitable for high-throughput database operations, intensive web serving, or complex computational tasks due to its lower CPU and memory write benchmarks.
description: Amazon Web Services r5a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7571 processor. It delivers 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, paired with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate modest compute capabilities, with CPU and memory write speeds generally placing in the lower percentiles, though cryptographic performance remains average due to hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instructions. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio, the r5a.xlarge is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that are not CPU-bound, such as caching layers, small-to-medium database deployments, and general-purpose memory-heavy applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads with modest compute requirements.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance offering a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio on AMD EPYC processors for cost-conscious, memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Features 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM
Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor at 2.5 GHz
Delivers 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage
Average cryptographic performance via hardware-accelerated AES and SHA
Best suited for memory-intensive caching and small-to-medium databases
